architecture_summary: Hybrid RAG: pdfplumber+PaddleOCR extraction, structure-aware chunking, BM25+bge-m3 hybrid search with RRF fusion, reranker disabled, gpt-4.1-mini streaming generation over adaptive top-k chunks (up to 4), answer-aware grounding from cited source blocks, 3-tier null detection.

answers.answer: Under Article 28(1), each Partner is jointly and severally liable with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while they are a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all other Partners.
The maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights is $1,500 as per Article 10(7) in Schedule 3 of the Foundations Law.
The Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar, after consulting the President prior to such appointment or dismissal.
The application for permission to appeal in case CFI 057/2025 was refused, and the Defendant was ordered to pay the ClaimantвЂ™s costs of the application, to be assessed on the papers.

DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022 amended the Law on the Application of Civil and Commercial Laws in the DIFC, the Foundations Law, the Common Reporting Standard Law, and the Employment Law, as indicated in the amendment notes of each respective law.
ONORA
The Permission to Appeal Application in SCT 169/2025 was refused, the request for an adjournment of the Hearing was also refused, and there was no order as to costs.
The effective date for due diligence requirements is 31 December 2016 for Pre-existing Accounts and 1 January 2017 for New Accounts under the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018.
Article 2 states the Personal Property Law 2005 is made by the Ruler of Dubai.
Article 16(1)(c) requires an Employer to keep records of the Employee's Remuneration, both gross and net where applicable, and the applicable Pay Period.

The No Costs Application by the Claimant seeking variation of the Judgment's costs order was dismissed, with no order as to costs for the application, and the Claimant was ordered to pay AED 70,000 in costs to the Defendant pursuant to the Judgment.

Under Article 12(4) of the Common Reporting Standard Law, records must be retained for six years after reporting. Article 18(2)(b) of the General Partnership Law requires preservation of accounting records for at least six years or as prescribed in Regulations.

The DefendantвЂ™s application for immediate judgment and/or strike out was dismissed by Order dated 26 January 2026, and the Defendant was ordered to pay 80% of the RespondentвЂ™s costs totaling USD 155,879.50 within 14 days, with interest accruing if unpaid.
A Reporting Financial Institution must provide an English translation of records obtained or created in a language other than English upon request by the Relevant Authority under Article 12(5).
The DIFC Personal Property Law 2005 was enacted on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law. The exact date is not provided in the context.
The Permission to Appeal Application was refused, and there was no order as to costs.
The consolidated version of the Law on the Application of Civil and Commercial Laws in the DIFC was published in November 2024.

The Court dismissed the ClaimantвЂ™s Application for a Default Costs Certificate due to failure to comply with procedural requirements under Rule 40.5 RDC. The Claimant must bear its own costs of the Application.
The Permission to Appeal Application in case SCT 295/2025 was refused, and there was no order as to costs.

The Court of Appeal allowed the Appellant's appeal, set aside the previous order except as to the Second Part 50 Order (amended), and ordered the Respondents to pay the Appellant's costs of the appeal assessed at USD 60,541.25.

The context does not specify a minimum period for preserving accounting records for either DIFC-incorporated General Partnerships or Limited Liability Partnerships. Only maintenance and inspection obligations are detailed without a defined preservation timeframe.

The ASI Order is discharged with immediate effect. The DefendantвЂ™s Set Aside Application is granted, and the Claimant must pay the DefendantвЂ™s costs of the Set Aside Application on the standard basis.

The application for permission to appeal was refused, the request for an oral hearing was denied, and the applicant was ordered to bear its own costs. The applicant may not request reconsideration at a hearing.
The Defendants' application for immediate judgment was dismissed. The claim will proceed to trial alongside the main issues.
The DIFCA is responsible for administering the Employment Law and any Regulations made under it, as stated in Article 8.
The Foundations Law is administered by the Registrar as stated in Article 8 of the Foundations Law.
Article 9(1) empowers the Board of Directors of the DIFCA to make Regulations to facilitate the administration of, or further the objects of, the Foundations Law.
